# Feature: Request Editor Spacing Polish + Params/Headers Copy-Paste Relocation

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-011
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Reclaim wasted vertical/horizontal space in the request editor per the annotated screenshot (`.github/images/waveclient_req_page_margin_issue.png`, markers 1‚Äì4): tighten the editor chrome margins around the method/URL row and breadcrumb (1) and the response section (4), and move the Params/Headers Copy/Paste buttons from their standalone toolbar row into the table header's Actions cell (2)+(3) ‚Äî reclaiming a full row above each table. **Pure presentational change** ‚Äî zero behavioral changes; clipboard logic, tooltips, and disabled states are preserved exactly.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

None ‚Äî presentational only. Layout contract:

- **Copy/Paste placement**: inside `<TableHead>Actions</TableHead>` ([RequestParams.tsx:174](../../packages/core/src/components/common/RequestParams.tsx) and the equivalent in `RequestHeaders.tsx`), right-aligned next to the "Actions" label (flex row, label left, icon buttons right); icon buttons sized to fit the header row (e.g., `h-6 w-6`/`size={14}`) without increasing header height.
- **Margin scale**: reduce outer paddings to minimal Tailwind steps (`p-4` ‚Üí `p-2`/`px-3` class of changes) while staying visually balanced; consistent across Params/Headers/Body/Validation tabs and the response section. Commit `cd089e2` already started the left-margin pass ‚Äî this completes top/bottom and the response divider.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestParams.tsx` ‚Äî remove toolbar div, relocate buttons into Actions header cell
- `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestHeaders.tsx` ‚Äî same
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx`, web `RequestEditor.tsx` ‚Äî chrome margin tightening (method/URL row, breadcrumb header, response status bar, tab content padding)
- Shared core request/response section components (locate via the editors' imports ‚Äî e.g., response status bar, tabs content wrappers)
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/RequestParams.test.tsx`, `RequestHeaders.test.tsx` ‚Äî updated queries/snapshots
- Docs: none (visual polish; screenshot refresh optional under FEAT-012's sweep)

## 4. Dependencies

- None. (If FEAT-009's Send‚ÜîCancel toggle lands first, coordinate on the method/URL row markup to avoid churn ‚Äî purely a merge-order note.)

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Relocate Copy/Paste into the table header ‚Äî `RequestParams.tsx`

- [x] **Implementation**: Deleted the standalone toolbar `div` above the table. The same two buttons now render inside the Actions `TableHead` as a flex row (`Actions` label left + right-aligned icon group), downsized to header height (`h-6 w-6`, `size={14}`); `copyParams`/`pasteParams` handlers, tooltips ("Copy parameters"/"Paste parameters"), and the `copyEnabled` disabled state are untouched.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Added `RequestParams.test.tsx` ‚Äî copy/paste buttons found within the table header row (`within(getHeaderRow())`); copy disabled when no params; copy enabled once a param has a key; clicking paste runs the clipboard-adapter logic and appends parsed rows (behavioral pin); clipboard read failure surfaces an error notification.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None (no API change).
- **Verify**: ‚úÖ `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test` green (1727 passed); table sits one full row higher with the buttons in the header.

### TASK-002: Same relocation ‚Äî `RequestHeaders.tsx`

- [x] **Implementation**: Mirrored TASK-001 in `RequestHeaders.tsx` (identical structure; tooltips "Copy headers"/"Paste headers").
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Added `RequestHeaders.test.tsx` with the same assertions for headers (includes a `ResizeObserver` stub for the autocomplete header-name input under jsdom).
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None.
- **Verify**: ‚úÖ Tests green; Params and Headers tabs identical in structure.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 (establishes the header-cell pattern)

### TASK-003: Editor chrome + response section margin pass (markers 1 & 4)

- [x] **Implementation**: Tightened vertical padding to a consistent scale in **both** platform `RequestEditor.tsx` files: the method/URL toolbar row `py-2.5` ‚Üí `py-2` (marker 1), and the request **and** response tab-content wrappers `py-3` ‚Üí `py-2` (marker 4). The big marker 2/3 reclaim is delivered by the TASK-001/002 toolbar relocation. `cd089e2` had already completed the horizontal (`px-6` ‚Üí `px-2`) pass and the response status-bar divider (`py-1.5`).
- [x] **Unit Tests**: No class-based snapshot assertions were affected (presentational). Verified web `RequestEditor.test.tsx` still green.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None.
- **Verify**: ‚úÖ Both platforms (vscode webview + web) typecheck and render with the same spacing; consistent `px-2 py-2` scale across request/response tab content; no horizontal-scroll regressions (widths unchanged).
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002 (their reclaimed row affects the final vertical rhythm)

> **DEVIATION (out-of-scope blockers fixed to unblock verification):** While verifying both platforms render, the FEAT-009/FEAT-010 working-tree changes in `RequestEditor.tsx` carried a temporal-dead-zone crash ‚Äî `handleSaveShortcut`'s `useCallback` referenced `handleSaveRequest` in its dependency array before that `const` was initialized, crashing the editor on every render. Relocated the save-shortcut block to **after** `handleSaveRequest` in both platform editors. Also: (a) added the missing `useSaveShortcut` export to the web `RequestEditor.test.tsx` `@wave-client/core` mock, (b) fixed pre-existing vitest-4 `Mock` typing errors in `useSaveShortcut.test.tsx` that were blocking core's `build:types` (and therefore the web build's `@wave-client/core` types), and rebuilt `packages/core` so its `dist` types expose `useSaveShortcut`.
